title: AtlasStudio Capability Registry Entry
description: Machine-checkable definition of one entry in the Capability Registry described by atlas-core/capabilities/README.md ('Each capability should eventually have a JSON definition with...'). This schema formalizes that registry direction; it does not itself populate atlas-core/capabilities/, which is outside this document's allowed change paths. A future work order should add the actual per-capability JSON files there, validated against this schema.

id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier, e.g. architecture-review, rpg-maker-json, canon-design, matching the Initial Capability Examples in atlas-core/capabilities/README.md.

description.description: What work this capability covers, concretely enough that a human can judge whether a given work order needs it.

category.description: Grouping label, e.g. creative, architecture, implementation, review, governance. Open-ended: the registry is still emerging, so no fixed enum is imposed yet.

typical_deliverables.description: Concrete artifact types this capability tends to produce, e.g. 'design document', 'python module', 'RPG Maker event page'.

risk_notes.description: What can go wrong when this capability is applied carelessly, and what raises or lowers the risk_level a work order requiring it should carry.

verification_signals.description: Observable signals a human or tool can check to confirm this capability was actually exercised competently, e.g. 'tests pass', 'canon linter reports no new findings', 'playtest confirms the described player experience'.

human_only.description: True for capabilities no AI provider may supply regardless of confidence score - final-canon, creative-direction, risk-acceptance, scope-approval, merge-approval - per atlas-core/orchestration and studio/orchestration/capability-based-orchestration.md's 'Human As Provider'. When true, every provider except provider.human is excluded during assignment, matching the canon_decision task class handling in studio/agent-assignment-model.md.
